reviews.review_description:
Ok. I'm quite new to VR and I am trying to be really supportive of lots of it as much as I can. I want to give good feedback and good reviews to support, but I also want to be honest.
I've been playing a lot of Les Mills which is awesome! But I wanted to switch things up a bit and I hate the subscription models, so single payment games (i refuse to call them apps) are what I prefer. Out of all the others available I gravitated to this one, as I liked the idea of it and it is a decent price and not too big of a file size.
Now the positives...I really like the idea of this, of being above scenic landscapes and turning 360 degrees to face oncoming targets from all directions. That is a great premise and novel too for VR! The scenery is stunning, and their is a decent variety of moves too.
There is a fair amount of journeys that range between 10 to 30 minutes each. Difficulty settings of lite, standard and intense adds variety too. There is some cool music, though I wonder if some is AI. But that's the thing now, not knowing. All uses of AI should be full disclosure because I hate it and hate what it is doing to society and everything at such a devastating and gaslighting rate.
And that's part pf my issues. Now the potential of this game is great, it does gamify the workouts which is cool. It diversifies and goes to the songs well. But it focuses too much on timing with the rhythm and not enough on helping with form. So targets come at you in an impractical and sometimes impossible way. They go to the beat but swarm you like chaos which makes you have to flail to do impossible transitions. This is quite dangerous because it can make you more likely to pull something. I have been stretching thoroughly when doing these but it leaves me aching in a way like I've pulled something rather than from a good workout.
It's cool to change direction, but too often the moves come at you right in your face so you can't react. Sometimes with that impossible move transition from the previous target that is just impractical to go for. I'm doing the lite mode as well. I'm trying to get used to it but there will be sets that go nicely and then they just throw everything at you because the song seems to demand it rather than your workout. I appreciate it is an everything workout but especially in lite mode it needs to tone it all down. Which makes me worry that the workouts are orchestrated by AI and not given safety checks by a human. All the moves seems like they were made by something that doesn't have to care about human bodily limitations and stresses, it just goes "this is a cool and varied combination of moves" without caring whether they are achievable or not. And that is the trouble. Maybe it isn't AI but it certainly feels like it. So AI is good for corner cutting but not for genhine experiences. It also isn't about improving AI to better the experiences, it is about not using it and instead taking the time and consideration as humans to create solid experiences for each other. So these workouts are hollow. I hear it is a single developer which I appreciate and admire, but if your workouts are going to be soulless amalgamations then that isn't an achievement.
I try to take this for what it is but it just isn't practical. Don't get me started on the frustratingly pointless "bonus stages" after the journey that abruptly end if you miss one of the chaos of targets that fly at you. I don't bother with those stages now.
I want this to win. I love the idea! Maybe it's abandoned now? I don't know. But if not, please tweak the actual workouts, don't rely on AI, get people to stress test the workouts and manage them so they aren't such chaos, and then you will have an amazing fitness game! This has so much potential

This is a great fitness app with some really catchy tunes. It won't make you a great boxer or anything, but you'll have a lot of fun breaking a sweat and the visuals are solid. That said, recently the multiplayer experience has become absolutely abysmal. It's confusing and glitchy and I can't for the life of me figure out how to consistently get my partner and I into the same match even though we're standing in the same room. The avatars are stuck in old school mode, and even choosing one of the defaults rarely sticks. There's an area where you can see your friends online, but you can't just select them to invite them to a match. Please, Dev, fix this. Yes, it would be great to have more songs for a 20 minute routine, but the multiplayer features desperately need an update.

reviews.review_title: The absolute greatest music rhythm game
reviews.review_description: A generous free demo version of HitStream is available on the Meta Quest store. HitStream is the absolute greatest music rhythm game on Meta Quest. No other game gives you the quality of music that HitStream does. No other game gives you the quality of gameplay that HitStream does. No other game allows you the level of gameplay-customization that HitStream does. Want an intense workout where targets come at you from three surrounding gates and you have to dodge and squat obstacles as you punch to the beat? You can do that. Want a concentrated boxing experience with no squatting, little dodging, and targets come from one single gate in front of you? You can do that too (even when seated, which aligns with the user's position great)! HitStream's large in-game soundtrack features both instrumental tracks as well as full-vocal, commercially-viable music (it should be noted that whether the music be full-vocal or just instrumental, the quality of music in HitStream is studio-grade, which is important to mention given how many other music rhythm games out there currently rely on AI-generated BGM-style music, which often feels soul-less and empty). The charting and timing of targets to hit as it relates to the music being played is excellent. The photo scape backgrounds are beautiful. The small details that prevent HitStream from attaining 5-stars in this review are A) No way to preview music tracks or to play individual tracks, as music is grouped into playlists called 'journeys' - the best the user can do is a random mode where the user chooses the music genre, the difficulty, and the amount of songs to play and then they hope that their favourite track comes up. B) There is no way to be 'invisible' if an internet connection is present, so other users who run the game can see when you are online in this game. C) Multiplayer mode places your avatar, shown mirrored, in the distance in front of you as you play, which is terribly distracting.

Hitstream is a rock-solid fitness game that truly ticks all the boxes. You’ll punch, swipe, and dodge through a wide variety of movements — and each level adds extra depth to keep things challenging and fresh. The music is perfectly synced to the gameplay, the sleek environments boost immersion, and the overall presentation is top-tier.

There’s a ton of content to work through, plus a multiplayer mode that’s a fantastic bonus for anyone wanting to train with friends. Whether you're a fitness enthusiast or just getting started, this is an unmissable app. Very impressed!

I've played a lot of fitness games and this is, hands down, the best I've ever played. It has the right amount of *everything*. I'll definitely be playing this on a regular basis as part of my daily workout routine.

Waiting for a long time, finally it is here and it exceeds expectations. Great workouts, great music, love it!

3 months in playing and still loving it a lot. There are a few things I am thinking about: love some moves which are very rare that really mix it up eg. catch a glow orb on opposite body side, or (how shall I describe to make this clear...) right hand hook, left hand follows the flow and hits out to the side, then the other way around.  left hand-> right hand -> then right hand <- left hand <-  . 

I play the lower levels with wrist weights .5kg per side, wish there were more songs with lower pace, as this slows down the punches a bit but gets a better workout.

Update, I am playing less snd less because there is not enough content, got boring with the same music and journey. The new journey 4 came 6 months ago, it said more journeys coming soon,  hope to get new content.

If you are into VR workout this is a must have as it is obvious that the creators have put a lot of effort behind it. The visuals are great, the songs are easily the best workout songs out there and the workout itself definitely makes you sweat.
However i find myself looking weird many times with some movements especially the swipe ones as i don't always understand what's the point of moving my hands in such a weird way especially when this has to happen extremely fast. I am playing in intense mode so not sure if that also applies to other modes. Either way since my goal is to lose weight i am not a huge fan of trying to figure out how to do such challenging dance movements. 
That being said i am giving 5 stars to support the great effort as i can live with the issues and i am enjoying the game so far.

This is our workout app of choice! Where so many workout apps are dark and gritty in their aesthetic, Hitstream is simply bright and beautiful. When I can't go outside to exercise in nature, I stay inside and play Hitstream.

It's fun to compete against your all-time high score, but let's be real: some days, you just can't compete with where you were a month ago. I love the 'multiplayer' option because you can compete with recent scores from other players (without the awkwardness of having our avatars in the same room). It's an absolute thrill to pick a nemesis and try to catch up to them!

developer_response.body: Hi @Sunsetfirefly16 - Thank you for the amazing review! As devs it's brilliant to hear that our players are truly enjoying the game! :)

reviews.review_description:
When a notification pops up it won't go away, will stay in your screen when playing. Press the menu button and it will not respond to anything anymore. Had to shut down by holding down the power button twice already.
So this is ruining my game play. Thought it was a good game but it doesn't work properly anymore.

developer_response.body: Hi @M_Nomz - Thanks for notifying us. This is the first report we've had of this – we'll look into it with Meta.

reviews.review_description:
Good workouts.
How can I get list of songs?

I use VR exclusively for fitness. This app is my go to. When I don’t feel like working out, Hitstream is still my favorite. When I’m motivated I enjoy seeing how how this old man can get on the top score lists. The songs are addictive. I hear them in my head all day, and enjoy them. The scenery is incredible. This is THE BEST fitness app by far. I use intense mode and really break a sweat and get a good cardio hit.

developer_response.body: Hey @Poppy3515 - Thank you for your support – wonderful to hear you're enjoying the game so much! We've heard the songs a million times now, and we're still not bored of them :)

reviews.review_title: It's good but could be better
reviews.review_description:
This has so much potential to be better with just a few extra options - so I really like this however.... I find myself looking for another app to fill the in gaps that this doesn't....

There really doesn't seem to be much difference between the light stage and intense stage - which means when you just want to warm up - your still going quite fast - and when you really want to go for it - your still going quite slow

It would be amazing to have a section just to warm up which doesn't require punching 

I also think it's very top heavy and very rarely do I squat in this which is missing half my body - it would be great to have more squats 

That being said, I do enjoy this, with the adjustments above it would be absolutely perfect and would be all in one - however without the above I'm having to double up on apps to look for the above

developer_response.body: Hi @1000moretimes - thank you very much for the review and the feedback. Great to hear you're enjoying the game!

reviews.review_description: I use 5-6 different fitness apps, and Hitstream is the one I use every time I work out. It's clear that a lot of passion went into the project from the developer. Great work 👍

developer_response.body: Hey @Ian.Frank - Thanks so much for the kind words – really appreciate the support.

reviews.review_title: Worst of all the exercise apps
reviews.review_description:
I use my Quest primarily for active, exercise apps like Les Mills Bodycombat and Powerbeats VR but over time they all get a little stale (not a slight against those great games). I needed something new and fresh.

This ain't it. There's some good ideas like crouching or leaning to have your head inside a target zone, but every move is so poorly executed and workout layouts are terribly designed.

The head movements are so unnatural, feedback with targets is very low and imprecise, and movements are all over the place and doesn't feel like you're working out. It feels more like you're frantically flailing out of rhythm with the music (which also isn't good) and I'm more likely to get a cramp or accidentally pull a muscle due to some random, jarring movement.

This is a half-baked exercise app and the worst of any I've tried. No idea how there's so many good reviews unless many of those people never tried any others.

developer_response.body: Hi @Hi.NotYou.Hi. - Thank you for trying Hitstream.

reviews.review_description: I can't praise this game enough. I find it fun, addictive and such great exercise. I'm new to VR and this is the 1st game I've bought on Quest so a great choice. I'm using MR as I feel safer that way (and so does the dog !). As a fit 70 year old, it's great to get the activity that I want and have fun in the process.

developer_response.body: Wow @viv.940468 – that's fantastic! So glad you found us! Happy to hear you can keep an eye on the dog too! :)

reviews.review_description:
I have played every fitness game on the app and have karate/boxing experience so I will be using this to guide my review.

Pros:
-The movements you do in this game feel varied and interesting. You tap orbs, you dodge obstacles, you punch blocks (and the direction you need to punch in changes). The beats match the music very accurately. 
-beautiful environments. They are very nature based


Cons:
-Weird AI/monotone sounding voice 
-No breaks between songs 
-no song or genre choice (only in one level) and the songs don't seem to match the genres very closely. For example, I chose electronic, dance and hip hop for my genre choices in the one level that allows it and the song they played sounded more indie/pop.
-no stationary mode/you must play in 360 mode
-standard mode is NOT standard and is more difficult than it should be. Even for me who is more experienced with martial arts. Lite mode is a good starter. I can't imagine what hard mode is like.

I don't think this game deserves a 4.8 rating, maybe a 4.0 at best. But in comparison to a few other fitness games, I think this is certainly better than Les Mills and PowerBeats. Les Mills is too repetitive and also lacks some customization. PowerBeats gameplay is just meh to me and probably the worst fitness game there is. 

However the truth is, the only workout game i have ever loved is FitXR. FitXR is very comprehensive and includes coaching, many different forms of exercise, more options for changing workout lengths, better music, etc. Audio Trip is another good non subscription option that, although lacking environment change, gives you song choice (songs you actually probably know), looks futuristic, and is nice intensive exercise.

developer_response.body: Hi @zarzsx – thanks very much for leaving a review. We're really pleased to hear you're enjoying the game. 360' rotation can be disabled in the preferences if you prefer more stationary gameplay. Thanks again!

reviews.review_description: I would like to learn how to play this game

developer_response.body:
Hi @Connie12555, you'll find the interactive tutorial as you start the game. However if you missed it, you can replay by selecting the 'player' icon at the bottom of the main screen, then selecting the 'manual' icon at the top right of the menu. 
Hope this helps get you started, if you have any trouble please join our discord and we would be more than happy to help out!

reviews.review_title: Sweaty FUN...We use it every day!
reviews.review_description: Each scene set has 10,20 and 30 min sets to choose from. The music is GREAT, upbeat, and you won’t get bored! It’s my husband and my go-to workout app...we use it every single day. Definitely worth getting!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review AileenO! Very happy to hear you guys are enjoying playing Hitstream :)

reviews.review_description:
When I fired up the demo I thought to myself, “meh, this looks like another cookie cutter rhythm/fitness game.” I’m glad I gave it a chance as I was soon pleasantly surprised and completely wrapped up in enjoying the workout. 

I found it much more engaging (an important word when it comes to VR titles) and enjoyable than the other fitness apps I have tried. The movements are varied, the changes in direction keep you thinking on your feet, and it does a good job of making sure your lower body isn’t left out. I really appreciate the length of the workouts, too. I can definitely see myself doing the 20 minute workouts on my lunch break at work. 

The demo is very generous, so if you are looking for a fitness app give this one a shot. Hopefully you’ll decide to support this title by buying the full version. It definitely should be out of the app lab and in the full Quest store - there are inferior fitness apps getting all the attention there and more people need to know about this one. 

Good job to the devs, hopefully they keep expanding the product and stick with the subscription-free model.

developer_response.body: Hey Wauregan - what a review! Thank you! You'll be pleased to hear that we have no plans to go down the subscription route. Keep an eye out for more content and features as we continue to expand the game. Thank very much for your support :)

reviews.review_description: I am only just getting into VR and honestly this is the best so far.  So probably not the most reliable review being I am a newbie but considering I spend most of my day in front of my laptop this has me up and working out in what for some are smoke breaks and lunch time.   I am going to suggest we have vr meeting rooms in the office for exactly this purpose.  Just brilliant!  Only thing I would suggest is a 'practice' mode for the scenes, for the less fit to ease in maybe.

developer_response.body: Hi contrarymary316 - what a great way to escape from work for a bit! Welcome to the wonderful world of VR! We do have plans to introduce a 'lite' mode for those who want a gentler workout, whether that's because they're new to VR workouts or they simply don't want to get too sweaty! Thanks for the fab review.
